name: rapidpro-python

metadata.name: rapidpro-python
metadata.summary: Python client library for the RapidPro API

documentation:
RapidPro Python Client
======================

[![Build Status](https://github.com/rapidpro/rapidpro-python/workflows/CI/badge.svg)](https://github.com/rapidpro/rapidpro-python/actions?query=workflow%3ACI)
[![Coverage Status](https://codecov.io/gh/rapidpro/rapidpro-python/branch/main/graph/badge.svg)](https://codecov.io/gh/rapidpro/rapidpro-python) 
[![PyPI Release](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/rapidpro-python.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/rapidpro-python/)

Official Python client library for the [RapidPro](http://rapidpro.github.io/rapidpro/). Supports latest Python 3.

Visit [here](http://rapidpro-python.readthedocs.org/) for complete documentation.

Installation
------------

```
pip install rapidpro-python
```

Example
-------

```python
from temba_client.v2 import TembaClient
client = TembaClient('rapidpro.io', 'your-api-token')
for contact_batch in client.get_contacts(group='Reporters').iterfetches(retry_on_rate_exceed=True):
    for contact in contact_batch:
        print(contact.name)
```

If you don't know your API token then visit the [API Explorer](http://app.rapidpro.io/api/v2/explorer)

Development
-----------

For discussions about future development, see the [RapidPro Developers Group](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!forum/rapidpro-dev).

To run the tests:

```
nose2 -C --coverage temba_client
```

functions.name: temba_client.utils.parse_iso8601
documentation: Parses a datetime as a UTC ISO8601 date

functions.name: temba_client.utils.format_iso8601
documentation: Formats a datetime as a UTC ISO8601 date or returns None if value is None

functions.name: temba_client.utils.request
documentation:
For the purposes of testing, all calls to requests.request go through here before JSON bodies are encoded. It's
easier to mock this and verify request data before it's encoded.

documentation: Base class for objects returned by the Temba API

classes.name: temba_client.serialization.TembaField
documentation: Field types

classes.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient._post
documentation: POSTs to the given endpoint which must return a single item or list of items

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient._delete
documentation: DELETEs to the given endpoint which won't return anything

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient._request
documentation: Makes a GET or POST request to the given URL and returns the parsed JSON

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient._build_params
documentation:
Helper method to build params for a POST body or query string. Converts Temba objects to ids and UUIDs and
removes None values.

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseClient._build_id_param
documentation: Helper method for case where an endpoint (e.g. a v2 update) requires a single identifying param (usually UUID)
Abstract base client

classes.name: temba_client.base.CursorIterator
documentation: For iterating through cursor based API responses

classes.name: temba_client.base.CursorQuery

methods.name: temba_client.base.CursorQuery.iterfetches
documentation:
Returns an iterator which makes successive fetch requests for this query
\:param retry_on_rate_exceed: whether to sleep and retry if request rate limit exceeded
\:param resume_cursor: a cursor string to use to resume a previous iteration
\:return: the iterator
Result of a GET query which can then be iterated or fetched in its entirety

classes.name: temba_client.base.BaseCursorClient

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseCursorClient._get_query
documentation: GETs a result query for the given endpoint

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseCursorClient._get_raw
documentation: GETs the raw response from the given endpoint

methods.name: temba_client.base.BaseCursorClient._request_wth_rate_limit_retry
documentation: Requests the given endpoint, sleeping and retrying if server responds with a rate limit error
Abstract base client for cursor-based endpoint access

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.get_archives
documentation:
Gets all matching archives
\:param str archive_type: "message" or "run"
\:param str period: "daily" or "monthly"
\:param datetime before: created before
\:param datetime after: created after
\:return: archive query
Fetch object operations

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.get_boundaries
documentation:
Gets all administrative boundaries
\:return: boundary query

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.get_contacts
documentation:
Gets all matching contacts
\:param uuid: contact UUID

\:param group: contact group name or UUID

\:param reverse: whether to return contacts ordered in reverse (oldest first).
\:param datetime before: modified before
\:param datetime after: modified after
\:return: contact query

documentation:
Gets an export of specified definitions
\:param flows: flow objects or UUIDs to include
\:param campaigns: campaign objects or UUIDs to include
\:param dependencies: whether to include dependencies
\:return: definitions export

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.get_runs
documentation:
Gets all matching flow runs
\:param uuid: flow run UUID
\:param flow: flow object or UUID
\:param contact: contact object or UUID
\:param responded: whether to limit results to runs with responses
\:param datetime before: modified before
\:param datetime after: modified after
\:param reverse: whether to return results ordered in reverse (oldest first).
\:param paths: whether to include path data
\:return: flow run query

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.create_broadcast
documentation:
Creates and sends a broadcast to the given URNs, contacts or contact groups
\:param str text: message text
\:param list[str] urns: list of URN strings
\:param list contacts: list of contact objects or UUIDs
\:param list groups: list of group objects or UUIDs
\:return: the new broadcast
Create object operations

documentation:
Creates a new campaign

Creates a new contact

methods.name: temba_client.v2.TembaClient.create_flow_start
documentation:
Creates a new flow start
\:param str flow: flow UUID
\:param list[str] urns: URNs of contacts to start
\:param list[str] contacts: UUIDs of contacts to start
\:param list[str] groups: UUIDs of contact groups to start
\:param bool restart_participants: whether to restart participants already in this flow
\:param bool exclude_active: whether to exclude contacts currently in other flows
\:param * params: a dictionary of parameters to pass to the flow
\:return: the new label
